title: Shadow classification recording
goal: Implement AF-04 from docs/roadmap/auto-frontier/05-shadow-mode.md. Give autonomy_level=shadow its read-only meaning: classify pending add_followup_slice proposals at existing replan checkpoints, record tier/reason-code annotations and events, and report would-have-promoted/agreement metrics. Never mutate queues, slice files, or decisions.
acceptance: Shadow runs classify pending add_followup_slice proposals at existing replan checkpoints and record tier, reason codes, envelope hash, budget snapshot, and classified_at without recording decisions.
Status/feed/report/handoff render shadow annotations and frontier agreement metrics from shared projection/read model wording.
off-level runs do not classify and remain behaviorally unchanged; promote/run levels before AF-06 behave as shadow/recorded-not-active only.
Tests prove .workflow/slices and remaining queue are byte-identical between off and shadow for the same candidates.
Resume/restart reclassification semantics are durable and event history is preserved.
must_ask_if: If the implementation would create a second proposal, decision, or apply authority channel outside the existing replan mechanism, stop and ask.
If required runtime behavior cannot be implemented within the declared areas, stop and ask rather than widening scope silently.
If a dependency or prerequisite from docs/roadmap/auto-frontier/00-matrix.md is not satisfied, stop and report the blocker.
If shadow mode would block on Tier-3 or mutate any queue/slice/decision state, stop and ask.
